interactions.description: Combined use significantly increases bleeding risk. Requires physician review before continuing both.
NSAIDs like ibuprofen increase bleeding risk when taken with warfarin.
Furosemide can affect blood sugar control, requiring closer glucose monitoring.
Both raise potassium levels; combined use risks hyperkalemia, especially in elderly patients.
Clarithromycin raises simvastatin levels, increasing risk of muscle damage (rhabdomyolysis).
Furosemide-induced low potassium can increase digoxin toxicity risk.
Combined use raises risk of serotonin syndrome, a potentially life-threatening condition.
Amlodipine can modestly raise simvastatin levels; usually manageable with dose limits.
Beta-blockers like metoprolol can mask symptoms of low blood sugar (hypoglycemia).
Calcium can reduce levothyroxine absorption; recommend spacing doses by 4 hours.
